aesthetic: a grand old Edwardian theatre, long shut up and faded, and the occult warren of tunnels beneath it — faded gilt, dusty velvet, cobwebs and old playbills; atmospheric, theatrical and a touch spooky — sensational and melodramatic rather than grim, oppressive or genuinely menacing. The upper theatre is dry and dusty, NOT wet; dampness, weeping brick and standing water belong only to the cellars, tunnels and sewers far below, where the prose calls for them.

scenes.theatre-lobby: Interior of a vast, opulent Edwardian theatre lobby — a cavernous double-height entrance hall, far wider and taller than it is deep, the grand foyer of a once-prestigious theatre. An open central void rises the full two storeys to an ornate plastered ceiling. Viewed from the main entrance (the south wall, behind the viewpoint and out of frame) looking north across the floor toward the far wall. The floor is a plain, worn marble-flagged foyer floor in muted neutral stone tones — a finished hard floor, NOT bare ground or dirt; understated, not brightly patterned. The focal point, centred on the far wall, is a monumental imperial (double-return) staircase whose steps run beneath a worn carpet runner: one wide central flight sweeps up to a deep mid-level landing, where it divides into two symmetric flights that curve left and right and rise to land directly on a continuous balustraded gallery. That gallery is one unbroken balcony ringing all four walls of the upper storey — running back along both side walls and across the entrance wall above the main doors — open over the floor below, so the whole upper level reads as a wraparound balcony around the central void. One upper storey only, no higher tiers. On the mid-landing wall beneath the split hangs a large faded full-length portrait of a distinguished gentleman in an old-fashioned suit. On the far side of the upper gallery, a single set of tall closed double doors leads through toward the auditorium; the rest of the gallery is plain balustrade with no other doors. At ground level the lobby has exactly two doorways, both small, private, modest panelled doors flanking the staircase: one closed door in the wall to the left (northwest), one closed door in the wall to the right (northeast) — discreet and plain against the opulence of the hall, with no other ground-floor doors. A few tall theatrical playbill posters are pasted flat to the plaster, bold pictorial imagery faded but striking. Opulent gilt and grand proportions, long shut up — faded grandeur on a vast scale.
scenes.managers-office: A cluttered former manager's office, viewed from the southeast doorway looking across the room. A heavy wooden desk strewn with loose papers stands against the far wall, with more papers scattered across the floor. A closed metal wall-safe is set in one wall. Set into the floorboards is an open trapdoor, with the top of a wooden ladder protruding from it, descending into darkness below. A second doorway is set in the north wall.
scenes.basement: A cellar lit only by grey daylight seeping in from small street-level windows set high on one wall, viewed from across the floor. Stacked wooden boxes and a couple of large barrels are the only contents. Set in the ceiling is an open trapdoor, with a wooden ladder descending from it down to the floor. An old electrical panel with a switch is mounted on one wall.
scenes.outside-the-theatre: A run-down city street in a decayed slum neighbourhood at twilight, viewed from the kerb looking toward the theatre's shabby frontage. Litter is strewn across the cracked pavement and a slight mist hangs low. Boarded-up, deteriorating buildings line the street, which runs away east and west into the distance. An empty parking space gapes at the kerb.
scenes.cramped-hallway: A narrow, cramped service hallway, viewed looking north along its length, its walls lined with stacked boxes of junk that pinch the passage tight. A doorway is set in the south wall and another in the west wall; the hallway continues north.
scenes.tight-stairway: A narrow, winding little service stairway, viewed looking up its turn. It climbs upward toward the west and descends toward the east. Cramped.
scenes.centre-of-long-corridor: A long corridor panelled in dark, aged wood, running north and south, viewed looking along its length. A narrow little staircase descends to the east; a closed door is set in the west wall. Worn floorboards.
scenes.south-end-of-long-corridor: The south end of a long, dark wood-panelled corridor, viewed looking north up its length. A doorway in the south wall and another in the west wall. Worn boards.
scenes.music-room: A cluttered former music room, viewed from the east doorway looking in. Battered violin cases and tangled coils of instrument strings litter the floor. An upright piano has been pushed up against one wall. Disused.
scenes.prop-room: A storage room for stage props, viewed from the north doorway looking in: shelves crammed with assorted junk line the walls. A closed trapdoor is set in the ceiling overhead. Cluttered.
scenes.southern-end-of-the-attic: The cramped southern end of a dusty attic. Rough wooden studs and crossbeams obstruct the low space; cobwebs and thick dust coat everything. The attic continues north. An open trapdoor in the floor leads down.
scenes.attic-above-the-theatre: A dusty attic space over the theatre auditorium: bare crossbeams and a thinly plastered floor, cracks in the plaster glowing faintly with light rising from the auditorium far below. The attic continues south. To the north, mounted on a pair of sturdy crossbeams, stands a large winch with a hand crank; a thick cable runs from it down through a hole in the floor. Drifting dust.
scenes.attic-observatory: A bizarre attic observatory. Instead of a dome, the roof is a single, strangely patterned window. The exposed wooden wall-beams are set at unnatural, impossible angles, giving the unsettling impression of standing inside a large many-sided geometric solid. A large, oddly-shaped brass telescope stands in the centre of the room. An opening leads out to the south.
scenes.narrow-hallway: A narrow hallway where stacked boxes have collapsed into a large heap of junk, viewed looking north. A pile of fallen carpet rolls lies tumbled across the passage, blocking the way north. The way back lies south. Choked with debris.
scenes.end-of-hallway: The end of a narrow, box-lined hallway, viewed looking north. To the north is the theatre's back exit; the hallway continues south, and an office door is set in the west wall. Stacked boxes.
scenes.alley-courtyard: A small enclosed courtyard at the centre of a city block, hemmed in on every side by tall, blank buildings that wall out any escape. Large old stage doors stand in the south wall, long disused and useless. A small back entrance is set in the southwest corner. A closed metal rubbish bin stands in one corner. Grimy, open to the sky overhead.
scenes.staircase-landing: The camera stands ON the mid-landing — a flat platform — looking north at the north wall, which is dominated by a huge framed full-length portrait of a distinguished gentleman in an old-fashioned suit; within the painting he stands in a wood-panelled room with an oaken door and a large fireplace, its roaring fire casting flickering light, the panelled background drawn in a subtly wrong, unsettling perspective. From this landing the grand staircase SPLITS and climbs upward to either side — a balustraded flight ascends to the left (west) and a matching flight ascends to the right (east), each rising straight up and out of frame toward the upper level, not curving or wrapping back on itself. These two ascending flights are the main subject. The staircase back down to the lobby (south) is behind and below the viewpoint: show at most the topmost couple of steps at the very bottom edge of the frame, or none at all — do NOT frame the whole descending flight and do NOT place the camera at the foot of the stairs. The only ways out are the stairs themselves: no doorways, arches, or dark openings cut into the walls. Faded gilt.
scenes.secret-gallery: A cramped hidden gallery, empty but for rows of old framed pictures crowding every wall. A small door leads out to the south.
scenes.eastern-landing: An upper landing that circles the balustraded rail above the theatre lobby, open over the lobby floor below. The landing continues south and west toward the stairs down. A set of double doors in the north wall lead toward the auditorium; a single, finely carved wooden door is set in the east wall. Faded gilt.
scenes.southern-landing: The southern end of the upper landing sweeping around the theatre's entrance hall, above the main doors and open over the lobby below. The landing continues to the northeast and northwest. Heavy closed curtains hang against the south wall.
scenes.eastern-theatre-aisle: A long aisle running north–south through a vast, wide theatre auditorium, flanked on both sides by long rows of many seats stretching far across the hall — a broad sweep of seating, NOT a narrow few-seats-wide strip. Viewed looking north up the aisle toward the far end, where a wide wooden stage stands raised above the auditorium floor, a sunken orchestra pit in front of it — a recessed trench between the stage lip and the front rows, visible from down here on the auditorium floor. A second, parallel aisle runs north–south through the seats away to the west. Balcony boxes line the raised east and west side walls, overlooking the seating. A great crystal chandelier hangs high over the centre of the auditorium (its normal raised position, not lowered). Behind the viewpoint, at the south end of the aisle, a set of double doors leads out. Carpet runs the length of the aisle.
scenes.eastern-stairway: A finely carpeted stairway, its once-rich runner now worn, viewed looking up its rise. It descends to a wooden door and climbs upward to the north. Enclosed.
scenes.eastern-balcony: You are in a private balcony box, looking out over its balustrade across the cavernous auditorium. Faded plush seats, set for the former elite, fill the box. Beyond the rail the great void opens out: rows of derelict seats far below, and a great crystal chandelier hanging out over the open centre. Across the void, the matching balcony boxes are just visible. A finely-sculptured plaster ceiling spans overhead. The only way out is a low gap at the edge of the box floor — no doorway.
scenes.western-balcony: You are in a long-abandoned private balcony box, every surface under a thick layer of dust. The wall fills the frame ahead: a doorway has been bricked solid, and beside it a small ragged hole has been knocked through the wooden wall, just large enough to crawl through, opening onto darkness beyond. The box floor is a faded, dust-greyed carpet — a finished carpeted floor, not bare ground, dirt or rubble. A couple of old plush balcony chairs, set for the former elite, still stand here, shrouded in dust and cobwebs. A finely-sculptured plaster ceiling spans overhead. Behind the viewpoint, the open balcony rail overlooks the auditorium void.
scenes.back-wall: The cramped, filthy interior of a hollow space within the wall dividing the theatre from the building beside it. Bare, dirt-caked timber and brick press close on all sides; almost no light reaches in. The hollow continues south, and a ragged hole opens to the southeast.
scenes.wall-with-large-hole: A narrow hollow within the dividing wall, continuing north. A large, ragged hole has broken through to the west, opening into the neighbouring building. Dirty, confined.
scenes.secret-library: A hidden library: rows upon rows of tall bookcases packed with hundreds of old books. Aisles lead away to the south; a small recess opens to the north. To the east a bookcase has collapsed, leaving a ragged hole broken through the wall. Enclosed.
scenes.reading-recess: A small alcove that was once a reading nook: a single small reading table and a wooden chair are all that remain. The library continues to the south.
scenes.southern-end-of-the-library: The southern end of the hidden library, its bookcases draped and webbed with strange, glistening pale strands. A cold, damp breeze enters through a broken French window in the west wall; a library aisle leads north. The glistening strands all converge on a large nest of rotting, heaped-up books in the centre of the floor. Damp, decaying.
scenes.street-balcony: An old stone balcony high above a side street in the abandoned quarter of the city. The view below is of boarded-up shop fronts and ruined, derelict buildings. Tall windows lead back inside to the east; the rough outer wall rises overhead to be climbed. Weathered stone, twilight.
scenes.theatre-roof: High on the steeply sloping rooftop of the theatre, beside a large brick chimney. All around, the rooftops of the abandoned city stretch away under a deepening dusk sky. The roof falls away dangerously on every side; a safe route leads west toward the balcony, and the chimney mouth opens downward. Windswept.
scenes.guest-star-room: A small but opulent guest suite, its furnishings of markedly finer quality than the rest of the ruined theatre: a fine carved four-poster bed and rich, faded fittings. A fireplace is set in the west wall. A door leads out to the east.
scenes.sealed-off-office: A former office, all of its furniture removed but for a small fireplace set in the west wall, its sooty flue an open shaft that continues both up and down. A door that once led east has been completely boarded over. In the middle of the room stands an old-fashioned wooden coffin with brass fittings, resting on a mahogany stand.
scenes.boiler-room: A cramped boiler room dominated by a huge old iron boiler, its pipes snaking out through and gripping the walls. A shaft rises from the boiler as the only way up; there is no other exit. A large, dirty coal bin stands against one wall, and a closed, grime-covered wooden chest sits nearby. Sooty.
scenes.north-end-of-long-corridor: The north end of a long, dark wood-panelled corridor, which runs away to the south, viewed looking south down its length. A doorway is set in the north wall and another in the west wall. Worn boards.
scenes.costume-room: A crowded theatrical wardrobe room packed with racks upon racks of old costumes spanning every era, from Imperial Roman dress to World War I uniforms. Three costumed display mannequins stand among them — one dressed as a pirate, one as a cowboy, one as a gladiator. A doorway leads out to the east. Crowded.
scenes.sick-bay: A small former sick bay. The walls are covered with old, faded medical instructional posters. A single iron infirmary bed stands against one wall, and a closed medical cabinet is mounted nearby. A doorway leads out to the south. Clinical.
scenes.sealed-up-ticket-booth: A small room that has been sealed shut, its windows boarded over so that only thin blades of faint light leak through the cracks. In one corner lies a human skeleton in a ragged, collapsed heap. Cramped.
scenes.stage: A wide wooden stage, raised well above the auditorium floor, in the derelict theatre — draped in cobwebs, viewed from the back of the stage (the back-stage area is behind the viewpoint, to the north) looking south out over the auditorium. The elevated stage drops away at its front lip; the sunken orchestra pit lies directly below and just in front of that lip and is HIDDEN from this raised vantage behind the stage edge — do NOT show the pit. Below and to the south spreads a vast auditorium, a sea of decaying seats, with two parallel carpeted aisles running away through it (one to the southeast, a matching one to the southwest). Faded balcony boxes line the raised east and west side walls. A great crystal chandelier hangs high over the centre of the auditorium. At the far south end, where the aisles reach the rear wall, double doors stand as the way out — the rear exits are at the aisle ends, NOT a central door behind the seats. Cavernous.
scenes.back-stage: A chaotic back-stage area, a maze of compartments divided by sagging ropes, wooden supports and hanging curtains, cluttered with rolls of old painted canvas scenery backdrops. The stage doors to the north have been boarded over and the stairs that once climbed up have been torn down; the only way out is back south to the stage. Cluttered.
scenes.up-the-ropes: High above the stage, amid a tangle of dangling rigging ropes hanging down into the fly space. Across a narrow gap, a row of doors is set into the west wall — they once opened onto a landing long since gone. One door hangs close at hand.
scenes.dressing-room: A former dressing room; scuff marks on the floor are all that remain of its vanished furniture. A door leads out to the east. Empty.
scenes.orchestra-pit: A view from down inside a bounded, curved, sunken Victorian orchestra pit, in front of the stage; we stand at the bottom of the pit looking toward the stage. The stage rises ahead to the north with a tattered curtain hanging above it. The pit is a small sunken well with its own low panelled walls, and it does NOT run the full width of the stage. Set into the EAST wall of the pit — the wall on the RIGHT — is a single plain closed wooden door to the storage cupboards: a door in the pit wall itself, low and at pit-floor level. To the LEFT (the west side), beyond the end of the pit, a short flight of wooden steps rises from the raised auditorium floor up onto the stage. Along the near house edge runs a low curved rail, with the front-row stalls seats just beyond it at head height. The bare wooden pit floor is sunk below floor level and empty.
scenes.pit-cupboards: Dark, empty walk-in storage cupboards. The orchestra pit lies back to the west; a gap broken in the east wall opens to the east. Cramped.
scenes.dark-place: A pitch-dark underground space; only the faint suggestion of rough passages leading away to the west and northwest can be made out. Near-total darkness.
scenes.under-the-stage: A cramped, low crawlspace beneath the stage; thin blades of light filter down through the cracks between the boards overhead. The ceiling is so low one must stoop. Small bits of debris that have fallen through the cracks litter the ground. A passage leads off into darkness to the southeast. Confined.
scenes.cloakroom: A disused cloakroom; rows of empty coat hooks line the walls, now draped only with cobwebs. The way back to the lobby lies southwest.
scenes.secret-stairway: A narrow secret stairway climbing up and descending down, feebly lit by a few bare electric bulbs set into the ceiling. Confined.
scenes.underground-passage: A dank underground passage lined with old, weeping brick walls. The passage climbs away to the west and runs east to a heavy, locked iron door. Damp, cold.
scenes.above-the-pit: A huge, cavern-like chamber with a large rectangular pit sunk into its floor, like a drained indoor swimming pool. A passage leads west to an open iron door; down inside the pit, a passage leads off to the east. Vast, rough stone.
scenes.inside-pit: The bottom of the rough-walled pit; its uneven sides make an easy climb back up and out. A gloomy tunnel leads away to the east. Rough stone.
scenes.tunnel-junction: A junction in a little network of rough-hewn underground tunnels. One tunnel runs west back toward the pit; others branch away to the northeast, southeast, and southwest. Cramped, rough rock.
scenes.mine-shaft: A rough chamber resembling an old mine shaft, lit feebly by a single bare electric light. Crude rock walls; tunnels lead off in all directions. Raw stone.
scenes.deep-in-the-mines: A cavern deep in the mine workings, widened out, with signs of major past excavation. A large iron cage dominates the space. Rough rock walls.
scenes.western-end-of-sewer: The western end of a large brick sewage trench that runs away east into the dark. A passage leads northwest, back beneath the theatre. This end terminates at a large iron sluice gate, below which a drain descends further into the muck. Wet brick.
scenes.centre-of-sewer: The middle of a long brick drainage trench, which runs east and west. Above, to the north, a strange metal platform is fixed against the wall. Wet brick.
scenes.eastern-end-of-sewer: The eastern end of the drainage trench, where it dead-ends at a cluster of small, impassable pipe mouths. The trench runs back west; a tunnel leads off to the southeast. Wet, grimy brick.
scenes.rat-nest: A small tunnel running northwest to southeast, its floor thick with rotting sewage and animal droppings. The tunnel ends at an unusual stone archway to the southeast, beyond which lies only darkness.
scenes.metal-platform: A raised metal platform fixed high on the wall, giving a clear view down over the entire brick sewer trench to the south. A long, rusty lever extends down from the ceiling within reach. Grimy ironwork.
scenes.witchs-lair: A small, circular chamber of red brick, domed like an igloo. In the centre stands an old iron cauldron filled with a dark, bubbling liquid. Shelves line the curved walls, crowded with little vials and strange knick-knacks — a witch's secret laboratory. The only way out is back to the northwest through a stone archway. Against one wall stands a large bronze statue of a four-armed woman draped in flowing bronze robes; her elongated forehead bears four eye-sockets set in a diamond pattern, and each of her four hands grips a jewelled dagger.
scenes.western-landing: An upper landing that circles the balustraded rail above the theatre lobby, open over the lobby floor below. The landing continues south and east toward the stairs down. A set of double doors in the north wall lead toward the auditorium. Faded gilt.
scenes.western-theatre-aisle: A long aisle running north–south through a vast, wide theatre auditorium, flanked on both sides by long rows of many seats stretching far across the hall — a broad sweep of seating, NOT a narrow few-seats-wide strip. Viewed looking north up the aisle toward the far end, where a wide wooden stage stands raised above the auditorium floor, a sunken orchestra pit in front of it — a recessed trench between the stage lip and the front rows, visible from down here on the auditorium floor. A second, parallel aisle runs north–south through the seats away to the east. Balcony boxes line the raised east and west side walls, overlooking the seating. A great crystal chandelier hangs high over the centre of the auditorium (its normal raised position, not lowered). Behind the viewpoint, at the south end of the aisle, a set of double doors leads out. Carpet runs the length of the aisle.
scenes.old-hallway: A sinister, murky, dim hallway running from southwest to northeast, its bare walls pressing close and the air heavy and oppressive. Viewed looking northeast down its length into deeper gloom.
